2604.23472.title: Escher-Loop: Mutual Evolution by Closed-Loop Self-Referential Optimization
2604.23472.paper_summary: While recent autonomous agents demonstrate impressive capabilities, they predominantly rely on manually scripted workflows and handcrafted heuristics, inherently limiting their potential for open-ended improvement. To address this, we propose Escher-Loop, a fully closed-loop framework that operationalizes the mutual evolution of two distinct populations: Task Agents that solve concrete problems, and Optimizer Agents that recursively refine both the task agents and themselves. To sustain this self-referential evolution, we propose a dynamic benchmarking mechanism that seamlessly reuses the empirical scores of newly generated task agents as relative win-loss signals to update optimizers' scores. This mechanism leverages the evolution of task agents as an inherent signal to drive the evaluation and refinement of optimizers without additional overhead. Empirical evaluations on mathematical optimization problems demonstrate that Escher-Loop effectively pushes past the performance ceilings of static baselines, achieving the highest absolute peak performance across all evaluated tasks under matched compute. Remarkably, we observe that the optimizer agents dynamically adapt their strategies to match the shifting demands of high-performing task agents, which explains the system's continuous improvement and superior late-stage performance.
2604.23472.paper_summary_zh: 雖然最近的自主代理展示了令人印象深刻的能力，但它們主要依賴手動編寫的工作流程和手工設計的啟發式方法，這本質上限制了它們在開放式改進方面的潛力。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了 Escher-Loop，一個完全閉環的框架，將兩個不同群體的相互演化具體化：解決具體問題的任務代理和遞歸改進任務代理及其自身的優化代理。為了維持這種自我參照的演化，我們提出了一種動態基準測試機制，無縫重用新生成任務代理的實證分數作為相對勝負信號，以更新優化器的分數。這一機制利用任務代理的演化作為內在信號，推動優化器的評估和改進，而無需額外的開銷。在數學優化問題上的實證評估顯示，Escher-Loop 有效地突破了靜態基準的性能上限，在所有評估任務中實現了最高的絕對峰值性能，並且在相同計算下表現最佳。值得注意的是，我們觀察到優化代理動態調整其策略，以適應高性能任務代理不斷變化的需求，這解釋了系統的持續改進和卓越的後期表現。
2604.23472.author: Ziyang Liu et.al.
2604.23472.authors: Ziyang Liu, Xinyan Guo, Xuchen Wei, Han Hao, Liu Yang
